posts.name: Kent Comley
posts.cooked:
I must sound like an old fart but I remeber with fondness the old days when you could actually buy this wine and in vintages like 2001 you could get hold of decent volumes. Managed to snare some BM, Cras and this village Chambolle from 2001.

This to me is now as close to perfect for a village Burgundy. It is so immaculately balanced, dense and weightless and has this sweet and salty unami character. It has great length and nuance - sous bois, soy, florals, there’s real dimension to the wine. Just entering prime time, but plenty left in the tank. This may sound like hyperbole but I can’t recall drinking a better village wine.

posts.name: Jeremy Holmes
posts.cooked: You are an old fart!

posts.name: alan weinberg
posts.cooked:
You are an old fart!

and I’m sure that, with age, the abbreviation BM takes on new meaning.

posts.name: Jeremy Holmes
posts.cooked: Best Village wine I have ever had is the '78 Roumier Chambolle. In fact one of the best wines I have had full stop.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
Best Village wine I have ever had is the '78 Roumier Chambolle. In fact one of the best wines I have had full stop.

Of course, that had Cras in it…

posts.name: Dale Williams
posts.cooked: A bit after release Jay Miller (the one who posts here, not the one who briefly wrote for WA) organized a “It Takes A Village To Raise a Chambolle” offline. A great linep of village Chambolle. Roumier was clear fave (about $40 I think at time, we bought out NYC - and maybe most of country- by next morning), but Drouhin, Mugnier, Barthod, and others were excellent, one of my fave all time tastings

posts.cooked:

Nice memory Dale. Have you tried any recently? I do think that I need to check in on a few more of my 2001 village wines. It has always been a vintage that I have enjoyed.

posts.name: Dale Williams
posts.cooked: Had the Mugnier village around Christmas this year, lovely. Have had Drouhin and Roumier last few years, all drinking well

posts.name: Jeremy Holmes
posts.cooked:
Best Village wine I have ever had is the '78 Roumier Chambolle. In fact one of the best wines I have had full stop.

Of course, that had Cras in it…

It did indeed.

My second most memorable Village red was the 1978 Forey Père et Fils Vosne-Romanée. It was made by Régis Forey’s father and Régis reckons it was probably a blend of Echezeaux, Gaudichots and village Vosne.

I’m beginning to see a pattern here William. I like '78’s with declassified material in them!

posts.name: Kent Comley
posts.cooked: For a time Vogue Chambolle-Musigny had declassified Musigny in it IIRC, so that would be right up your alley Jeremy.

posts.name: Gaudissabois Johan
posts.cooked:
Dear William,

The Chambolle Roumier did not only have CRAS in it as you rightly observe but also COMBOTTEs and, if I am not mistaken, a bit FUEES as well.

SINCERELY JOHAAN

posts.name: Brady Daniels
posts.cooked:
For a time Vogue Chambolle-Musigny had declassified Musigny in it IIRC, so that would be right up your alley Jeremy.

Wasn’t that the Chambolle Premier Cru? Or did they also put some in the village?

posts.name: Gaudissabois Johan
posts.cooked:
Dearv Brady,

Declassified MUSIGNY by CdV indeed went into the premier cru. That is certainly the case. The best part of the younger vines that was. A very small part (the rest) , however, was also used in the village. So I was told. Total amount of this, however, is to be neglected.
SINCERELY JOHAN

posts.name: Joshua Kates
posts.cooked:
Yes, great note, Kent,

Thanks! I don’t get to drink as much Roumier as many on this board–or who post on this thread!–but when I do, I am always struck by their classicism: remarkable balance and purity. Somehow reminds me of 1st growth Bordeaux.

posts.name: Peter Chiu
posts.cooked: Roumier Chambolle Musigny used to be my go-to favorite village wine …but no more for the last few years - since you need to wait for the allocation and buy it from SAQ in the lottery system.

posts.name: A Songeur
posts.cooked:
Bought 69 from 2007 to 2017, drank 9 of them on the young side, all excellent, will need to wait a bit longer to taste them fully mature… Less so than for his Bonnes mares where my oldest bottle is 2008… I need to survive a few pandemies for these…

One need to buy many bottles to get a favorite…

posts.name: Jayson Cohen
posts.cooked:
Bought 69 from 2007 to 2017, drank 9 of them on the young side, all excellent, will need to wait a bit longer to taste them fully mature… Less so than for his Bonnes mares where my oldest bottle is 2008… I need to survive a few pandemies for these…

One need to buy many bottles to get a favorite…

That’s a nice stash. 1995 is my favorite, but I never tried the ‘78 to my recollection, haven’t tried 2001 since it was much younger, and stopped buying for my own cellar a long time ago. The ‘95 may be my favorite village wine ever.
